doctor_description: Increased size intrathoracic lymph nodes prevascular group up to 1.6cm short axis. No prior comparison made in findings.
validator_description: Interval worsening: image 47 node grew from 4mm to 1.5cm, image 80 from 4mm to 1.2cm; image 101 decreased to 1.1cm (previously 1.5cm). May be asbestos-related or neoplastic.
management_impact: Validator provides critical interval comparison showing dramatic growth (4mm→15mm) and attributes to asbestos or neoplasm. Doctor reports lymphadenopathy without quantified interval change or differential. No immediate management change but validator's framing is clinically more actionable.
notes: Validator's interval characterization (4mm→15mm growth) and etiological differential (asbestos vs neoplasm) are clinically important missing elements from doctor's report.

doctor_description: Areas of localized costal pleural thickening bilaterally up to 5mm with calcification. Not specifically attributed.
validator_description: Multiple calcified pleural plaques with interval increase in size of some calcifications — may represent asbestos-related pleural disease.
management_impact: Validator attributes calcified pleural plaques to asbestos-related disease and notes interval growth of calcifications. Doctor documents thickening without asbestos attribution. Clinically significant differential that links to lymphadenopathy etiology. No immediate imaging change.
notes: Asbestos attribution connects lymphadenopathy and pleural findings. Doctor's characterization lacks this clinical context.

doctor_description: Moderate coronary calcification. No PCP recommendation in impression.
validator_description: Moderate coronary calcification. PCP recommendation explicitly stated.
management_impact: ACRad44 PCP referral language absent from doctor's impression.
notes: ACRad44 MIPS violation — doctor documents CAC but omits PCP recommendation from impression.

doctor_description: Left lobe thyroid hypodense lesion 9×7mm. Impression: per ACR White Paper 2014, thyroid ultrasound is recommended.
validator_description: Left lobe thyroid hypodense lesion 9×7mm. Impression: per ACR White Paper 2014, no need for further follow-up.
management_impact: Doctor recommends thyroid ultrasound; validator correctly applies ACR 2014 White Paper — no follow-up needed for incidental thyroid nodule of this size. Doctor triggers unnecessary imaging. Measure 406.
notes: ACR 2014 thyroid incidentaloma guideline: <1.5cm in patients <35 y and <2.5cm in patients ≥35 y — no follow-up. Doctor's US recommendation is incorrect per guidelines.

doctor_description: Two left renal hypodense lesions noted — no mention of new lesion.
validator_description: Two left renal lesions; upper pole lesion is previously not seen (new). Per ACR White Paper 2018 no follow-up required.
management_impact: Doctor documents two lesions but doesn't note the upper pole one is new. Validator identifies interval change. Management is the same (no follow-up) but interval characterization is missing.
notes: New renal lesion not characterized as new by doctor. Same management conclusion but interval documentation gap.

validator_description: Common bile duct measures up to 12mm, unchanged.
management_impact: 12mm bile duct dilation is a reportable finding. Validator notes it is unchanged. Doctor omits entirely. No additional imaging triggered (stable) but documentation gap.
notes: Bile duct dilation omitted by doctor. Stable per validator but should be documented.

validator_description: Severe degenerative changes of shoulder joints.
management_impact: No management change — incidental musculoskeletal finding.
notes: Shoulder joint degeneration omitted by doctor. No clinical consequence.

doctor_description: No nodules requiring follow-up identified.
validator_description: Solid nodule 7mm (series 4, image 90). Adjacent solid nodule 4mm (series 4, image 93). Impression: per Fleischner CT 3-6 months, if stable no follow-up or optional CT 18-24 months.
management_impact: Doctor explicitly states no nodules requiring follow-up. Validator finds 7mm solid LLL nodule requiring 3-6 month CT follow-up per Fleischner 2017 (solid 6-8mm: CT 3-6m then 18-24m). Management change: follow-up CT required. Measure 364 violation.
notes: Critical miss: 7mm solid LLL nodule requiring Fleischner follow-up completely absent from doctor's report. Doctor's negative statement is directly contradicted by validator.

doctor_description: Bilateral diffuse mosaic attenuation — differentiate COPD, hypersensitivity pneumonitis without fibrosis, small airways disease (bronchiolitis).
validator_description: Bilateral diffuse mosaic attenuation with multiple air trapping — bronchial disease (inflammatory or infectious) with bronchial obstruction.
management_impact: Both identify mosaic attenuation. Validator adds 'air trapping' and narrows to bronchial disease/obstruction. Doctor offers broader differential. No management difference.
notes: Stylistic difference in differential framing. Same clinical finding, same management direction.

doctor_description: Mild coronary artery calcification. PCP recommendation stated.
validator_description: Mild CAC in LAD and RCA. PCP recommendation stated.
management_impact: None — ACRad44 satisfied by both.
notes: Both concordant on mild CAC with PCP recommendation.

doctor_description: Round lesion 6mm well-defined margins at splenic hilum — likely accessory splenule.
validator_description: Upper abdomen unremarkable.
management_impact: No management change — accessory splenule is benign, no follow-up. Validator says upper abdomen unremarkable. Minor overcall.
notes: Accessory splenule documented by doctor, validator reports abdomen as unremarkable. No clinical consequence.

finding_category: RLL perifissural nodule — wrong follow-up recommendation
doctor_description: Solid 6mm nodule along interlobar pleura image 123. Impression: CT follow-up in 6-12 months per Fleischner.
validator_description: Solid 6mm nodule along interlobar pleura image 123 — perifissural node, requires no follow-up.
management_impact: Doctor recommends 6-12 month CT follow-up for a perifissural node that requires no follow-up per Fleischner 2017. Unnecessary surveillance CT triggered. Measure 364.
notes: Perifissural nodes are specifically excluded from Fleischner follow-up. Doctor applies incorrect algorithm triggering unnecessary imaging.

doctor_description: 5mm solid nodule image 29. Stable. Impression: CT follow-up 6-12 months per Fleischner.
validator_description: 4mm solid nodule image 29, unchanged vs Dec 22 2025 and Apr 12 2024. Impression: no further follow-up per Fleischner.
management_impact: Doctor measures 5mm and recommends CT follow-up; validator measures 4mm and recommends no follow-up. Per Fleischner 2017: solid nodule <6mm in low-risk patient — no follow-up. Doctor triggers unnecessary CT. Measure 364.
notes: 1mm measurement difference leads to different Fleischner pathway. Validator's no-follow-up is correct for 4mm solid nodule. Doctor's 5mm measurement changes management.

doctor_description: Single areas of paraseptal emphysema noted in findings. Not mentioned in impression.
validator_description: Mild paraseptal emphysema. Impression: independent lung cancer risk factor — consider LDCT enrollment if eligible; today's CT may serve as baseline.
management_impact: Doctor documents emphysema in findings but omits QMM23 LDCT screening recommendation from impression. Validator explicitly includes it.
notes: QMM23 miss — emphysema documented but LDCT screening language absent from impression.

doctor_description: Severe coronary artery calcification in findings. Impression: item 4 mentions severe CAC only — no PCP recommendation.
validator_description: Severe CAC in all four main coronary arteries. PCP recommendation explicitly stated.
management_impact: ACRad44 PCP referral language absent from doctor's impression.
notes: ACRad44 MIPS violation — severe CAC documented but PCP recommendation absent from impression.

doctor_description: No cardiomegaly. No pericardial effusion.
validator_description: No cardiomegaly, mild enlargement of the left ventricle.
management_impact: No management change — incidental LV enlargement omitted.
notes: Mild LV enlargement noted only by validator. Incidental finding, no immediate action required.

doctor_description: Solitary solid pulmonary nodules up to 5mm. Per Fleischner no additional CT follow-up required.
validator_description: Solid nodules up to 5mm both lungs. Per Fleischner: low-risk no follow-up; high-risk generally no follow-up, optional CT 12m if high suspicion.
management_impact: None — both recommend no follow-up per Fleischner. Measure 364 satisfied.
notes: Concordant on nodule management. Both correctly apply Fleischner for ≤5mm solid nodules.

doctor_description: Sclerotic lesion T10 up to 8mm density ~440 HU. Impression: Bone-RADS 2 — correlation with other imaging required.
validator_description: Sclerotic lesion T10 up to 8mm density ~440 HU. Impression: listed without Bone-RADS classification, no follow-up recommendation stated.
management_impact: Doctor applies Bone-RADS 2 and recommends correlation with other imaging. Validator documents finding without classification or follow-up recommendation. Doctor's Bone-RADS 2 may prompt unnecessary additional workup for a likely benign bone island.
notes: Bone-RADS 2 classification by doctor triggers imaging correlation recommendation. Validator omits classification. The high density (440 HU) is most consistent with a bone island (Bone-RADS 1).

doctor_description: Moderate degenerative changes with small osteophytes.
validator_description: Mild degenerative changes with small osteophytes.
management_impact: No management change. Severity wording difference only.
notes: Moderate vs mild grading discrepancy — no clinical consequence.

doctor_description: 6mm solid nodule RLL — new vs July 2013 abdominal CT. Per Fleischner: low-risk CT 6-12m; high-risk CT 6-12m then 18-24m.
validator_description: 6mm solid nodule RLL — new vs July 2013. Same Fleischner recommendation.
management_impact: None — both concordant on Fleischner follow-up for new 6mm solid nodule. Measure 364 satisfied.
notes: Fully concordant finding and management.

doctor_description: Cholecystectomy noted. Hepatic steatosis noted. No mention of bile duct.
validator_description: Cholecystectomy. Bile ducts measure up to 1.7cm — not previously dilated.
management_impact: New bile duct dilation to 1.7cm in post-cholecystectomy patient is a clinically significant finding requiring workup (biliary obstruction, stones, stricture). Doctor completely misses this finding. Management change: biliary evaluation required.
notes: Critical miss: 1.7cm CBD dilation is new in a post-cholecystectomy patient. This is a management-changing finding requiring biliary workup.

doctor_description: No significant pericardial effusion.
validator_description: Trace pericardial effusion.
management_impact: Trace pericardial effusion — no management change. Minor omission.
notes: Trace effusion not mentioned by doctor. No actionable consequence.

doctor_description: Mediastinal lipomatosis.
validator_description: Epicardial lipomatosis.
management_impact: No management change. Both describe fat deposition; anatomical attribution differs (mediastinal vs epicardial) — same benign finding.
notes: Localization difference for same benign fat deposition. No clinical consequence.

doctor_description: Calcified subcutaneous lesion right breast 5mm. Impression: correlate with physical examination and mammography.
validator_description: Benign subcutaneous calcification right breast — was reported on mammogram in 2016. No recommendation made.
management_impact: Doctor recommends mammography for a calcification already documented on prior mammogram (2016). Validator correctly identifies it as known and benign — no follow-up needed. Unnecessary imaging recommendation.
notes: Known calcification from 2016 mammogram incorrectly prompts new mammography recommendation. Minor overcall.

doctor_description: Diffuse moderate hepatic steatosis.
management_impact: No management change — hepatic steatosis is incidental, no follow-up imaging required. Validator omits, doctor documents. Documentation difference.
notes: Doctor documents hepatic steatosis not mentioned by validator. No management consequence.
